name: BiteBuilder UX — Bite Cards, Siderail Cleanup, and Layout Polish
description: The copilot chat currently renders all assistant output as plain escaped text with inline [N] segment chips. When the model returns a structured bite recommendation block (bold header with label, title, and timecodes; italicised quote; 'Why it works' rationale), nothing distinguishes it visually from surrounding prose. This PRD makes bite blocks scannable by rendering them as blue cards directly in the chat, removes the model/timeout controls that clutter the siderail, and gives the layout more breathing room. All visual changes are verified with Playwright.

userStories.title: Render bite recommendation blocks as blue cards in the chat
userStories.description: As an editor, I want bite recommendation blocks in the assistant's reply to render as visually distinct blue cards so I can scan which clips are being suggested without reading every word of prose.
acceptanceCriteria: In renderChatMessageContent() in static/app.js, before the existing [N] chip pass, split the raw content string on the bite-block pattern: a line matching /^\*\*\[.+?\].*?\(\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}[:\;]\d{2}[–-]\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}[:\;]\d{2}\)\*\*/ followed by optional italic quote and optional 'Why it works' bullet.
Each detected bite block is wrapped in a <div class="bite-card"> element. Non-bite prose segments are passed through the existing [N] chip renderer unchanged.
The bite-card block renders three sub-sections: a header row with the label badge, title text, and timecode range; an optional quote line in italics; and an optional why-it-works line.
Add .bite-card CSS to static/app.css: blue-tinted background (#eaf2ff), 3px solid left border (#2458c4), 8px 12px padding, 6px border-radius, 8px bottom margin, font-size 0.9em.
The label badge inside .bite-card header uses a small pill: background #2458c4, white text, 3px 7px padding, 4px border-radius, uppercase, 0.75em font-size.
Existing [N] chip references that appear inside a bite-block quote are still rendered as interactive chips (the [N] pass runs on the inner text of each sub-section).

Install Playwright (npm init playwright@latest --yes or pip install playwright && playwright install) and write tests/playwright/bite_cards.spec.js (or .py) that: mock a chat message containing one bite block in the above format, assert a .bite-card element exists in the DOM, assert its computed border-left-color is rgb(36,88,196), assert the label pill text matches the label from the header.
Playwright tests pass (npx playwright test or pytest tests/playwright/).
Existing JS unit behaviour is unchanged: renderChatMessageContent still returns a string, still handles content with no bite blocks identically to before.
No Python/Flask changes required.

userStories.title: Remove model and timeout controls from the copilot siderail
userStories.description: As an editor, I want the siderail to show only project context — transcript name, brief summary, suggested plan, and accepted plan — so the panel feels focused on the cut, not on configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: In templates/copilot.html, remove the entire <section> containing the 'Model' heading, #modelSelect, #checkModelsButton, [data-model-status], #thinkingModeSelect, and #timeoutInput.

The siderail section that previously held the Model panel now shows only: the Transcript stat card, the Brief summary card, the Suggestions panel (#suggestedPlan), and the Accepted panel (#acceptedPlan).

In static/app.js, remove all lines that read from or write to modelSelect, checkModelsButton, thinkingModeSelect, and timeoutInput (getElementById calls, event listeners, syncInputValue calls, updateBindings calls). The state.model, state.thinkingMode, and state.timeout fields may remain on the state object for API compatibility but must not be bound to removed DOM nodes.
No JS console errors on page load after removal.
The copilot page still functions: chat sends, generation works, plan cards update.

Write a Playwright test in tests/playwright/siderail_cleanup.spec.js (or .py) that loads the copilot page and asserts: document.querySelector('#modelSelect') === null, document.querySelector('#timeoutInput') === null, document.querySelector('#thinkingModeSelect') === null.
Playwright tests pass.

userStories.title: Widen layout margins and increase chat breathing room
userStories.description: As an editor, I want the app to feel less cramped so I can read the chat and siderail content without the walls closing in.
acceptanceCriteria: In static/app.css, increase .app-shell padding from 16px to 24px and increase max-width from 960px to 1100px.
Increase .panel-grid gap from 12px to 20px.
Increase .message padding (top+bottom) from its current value to at least 12px top and 12px bottom.
Increase .chat-log padding (if any) or add padding: 0 8px so message text does not flush against the panel edge.
The responsive breakpoint at max-width: 900px should be updated to max-width: 1060px to match the new max-width.

Write a Playwright test in tests/playwright/layout_margins.spec.js (or .py) that loads the copilot page and asserts: getComputedStyle of .app-shell has paddingLeft >= 24px, getComputedStyle of the first .message has paddingTop >= 12px.
Playwright tests pass.
No visual regression on the intake, generate, or export pages (those use the same app.css but different templates — spot-check that they still load without layout breakage).

userStories.title: Playwright smoke suite covering the full copilot page load and chat render
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Playwright smoke test that loads the copilot page end-to-end and verifies the key UX changes introduced in US-001 through US-003 are present together, so regressions are caught in one run.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/playwright/copilot_smoke.spec.js (or .py) that starts the Flask dev server on an available port (or uses a fixture server), navigates to the copilot page, and asserts all of the following in sequence:
1. Page title or h1 contains 'BiteBuilder' or 'Copilot'.
2. #modelSelect is not present in the DOM.
3. #timeoutInput is not present in the DOM.
4. .app-shell computed paddingLeft >= 24px.

5. Inject a synthetic assistant message containing a bite block matching the pattern **[Label] Title (00:00:00:00–00:00:10:00)** followed by a quote and a Why it works line, trigger a re-render, and assert a .bite-card element appears with border-left-style solid.
Add a playwright.config.js (or pytest-playwright config) at the repo root with: baseURL pointing to localhost on the dev server port, 1 worker, headless: true for CI.
Add an npm script in package.json (create if absent) or a Makefile target: 'test:playwright': 'npx playwright test' (or equivalent for Python).
All Playwright tests in tests/playwright/ pass in a single run.
The existing Python unittest suite (.venv/bin/python -m unittest discover -s tests -v) still passes alongside the new Playwright suite.
